title: Comprehensive Protocol for Total Protein Extraction, Precipitation, and Assay Measurement from Plankton Samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9mbrc","text":"This protocol outlines an optimized method for extracting total protein from plankton samples, encompassing both phytoplankton and zooplankton. The procedure involves the use of lysing matrix tubes with beads of varying sizes (0.1 mm, 1.4 mm, and 4 mm), eight homogenizing cycles, and a protein extraction buffer composed of 2% SDS, 10% glycerol, 5 mM EDTA in 100 mM Tris (pH 7). The extracted crude protein is quantified using the Pierce Micro BCA assay equivalent to bovine serum albumin (BSA), with a linear detection range of 12.5 to 1000 ug/sample.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2h50l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f563o","text":"To remove interference from chlorophyll, monosaccharides and other substances, the extracted protein undergoes precipitation using a chloroform and methanol solvent mixture, followed by re-dissolution in re-solubilization buffer composed of 1% sarcosine in 50 mM Tris (pH 7). BCA assay is then used to quantify the precipitated protein, with BSA as the standard. The absence of glycerol and the use of sarcosine instead of SDS enable a linear detection range of 5 to 2000 µg/sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ldfe","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4pu7t","text":"https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/LSG/manuals/MAN0011430_Pierce_BCA_Protein_Asy_UG.pdf","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":95}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"8B9973430D7811EB96FE0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/LSG/manuals/MAN0011430_Pierce_BCA_Protein_Asy_UG.pdf"}}}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"elepu","text":"Waste from BCA assay needs to be collected into waste container and gets further treated before disposal due to the negative impact on the activity of microorganism.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4dpdt","text":"Solvent from protein precipitation (1) the mixture of water and methanol (2) the mixture of methanol and chloroform shall be disposed into separated waste container. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Microalgae samples

Filter microalgae in liquid media onto filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (130 mmHg). Samples collected on GF/F filters yield higher protein recovery compared to those on polycarbonate filters by using the same protocol.
Place folded filter in 2 mL cryogenic vial.
100 mM pH 7 Tris buffer Dilute 1 part 1M pH 7 Tris with 9 part MilliQ. Tris Buffer 1M pH 7.0 Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP1756-500
Prepare samples for homogenizing Zooplankton samples are already in the bead tubes. Phytoplankton samples need to be transferred from cryogenic tubes to bead tubes by following the steps below:
Remove samples out of the ULT and keep them On ice .
Turn on FastPrep FastPrep-24 5G (Bead beater, MP Biomedicals, 116005500, https://uk.mpbio.com/fastprep-24-5g-instrument )
Check the cap of each tube to ensure cap is tightly closed. Organize the tubes in order, log the position of each tube, in case the labels get rubbed out during extraction.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
De-foam by centrifuging the extract. 20000 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Transfer extract to 2 mL microtubes.
Freeze aliquoted extract and the remaining extract at -80 °C .
Organize eleven 2 mL microtubes in the tube rack, label the tubes from C1 to C11 (C denotes crude).
Use the following formula to determine the total volume of WR required. Consider leaving several mL of extra volume: (# standards + # samples) X 200 µL = total volume WR required
Prepare WR by mixing 200 parts of Micro BCA reagent A, with 192 part of Micro BCA Reagent B, and 8 part of Micro BCA reagent C in a 15 or 50 mL falcon tube.
Turn on incubator and preheat to 37 °C SHAKING INCUBATOR (71L, Corning® LSE™, 6753)
Shake for 5 s at 600 rpm in a continuous and high force mode Read endpoint 562 nm with a measurement time 100 ms Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)
Subtract the average absorbance at 562 nm of the blank standard replicates from the 562 nm measurements of all other individual standards .
Subtract the average absorbance at 562 nm of the blank sample (filter) replicates from the 562 nm measurements of all other individual samples .
Create a response curve where the corrected absorbance at 562 nm for each BSA standard is plotted against its concentration in mg/mL.
Use the standard curve to determine the protein concentration of each unknown sample by using its corrected absorbance at 562 nm.
Protein_mg/filter = Protein_mg/mL X PEB_mL X DF In Micro BCA assay, DF = 25 In BCA for precipitated protein, DF = 1

Reverse pipet, load 100 µL standard solutions into the microplate, in duplicate. Be aware of the layout, C12 (the blank) is loaded first. A B C D 1 C12 C12 C4 C4 2 C11 C11 C3 C3 3 C10 C10 C2 C2 4 C9 C9 C1 C1 5 C8 C8 6 C7 C7 7 C6 C6 8 C5 C5

Using Finnpipette stepper pipette (5 mL tip), dispense 100 µL of WR into each well. Ensure that the tip does not come into contact with the solution to prevent cross-contamination of samples. 96-Well Microplates (Polystyrene, Clear,, Greiner Bio-One, 82050-760)

Cover the microplate with lid, shake and incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00 at 150 rpm. Microplate Lids (Polystyrene, Greiner, 656170, https://www.fishersci.ca/shop/products/high-profile-polystyrene-microplate-lids-2/07000288#clear%20microplate%20lid )
Actual detection limit of the assay ( L-LOD)

Calculate the volume to obtain enough biomass for the assay: If using 500 uL extraction buffer, the minimum sampling volume (mL) = 750/(Chl-a_ug/L) If using 1000 uL extraction buffer, the minimum sampling volume (mL) = 2X750/(Chl-a_ug/L)
Zooplankton samples

Grind freeze-dried samples in metal grinding tube (need dry ice) Metal lysing matrix tube (MPBio, 116992006) CoolPrep™ adapter for 24 x 2 mL tube holder on FastPrep-24 (MPBio, 116002528) FastPrep-24 5G (Bead-beater, MP Biomedicals, 116005500, https://uk.mpbio.com/fastprep-24-5g-instrument , https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/public/39d9677dde602e4eb59e1a2a80692deb0a16d60c7dc1af5eefdc42b950e8922e/bnxwia8e.jpg )

Transfer ground sample into Lysing matrix tube (containing 1.4 mm ceramic spheres, 0.1 mm silica spheres and one 4 mm glass bead), weigh the biomass and log into sampling sheet. Lysing matrix E tube (2 mL, MPbio, 116914050-CF) Prefilled tubes (100 µm and 4 mm silica and 1.4 mm zirconia acid washed beads, Cole-Parmer, 2303-MM3)
Reverse pipet 1 mL PEB (1X) into each bead tube.
Rinse filter tunnel with filtered artificial seawater (nutrient free) to avoid sample loss.
Filter blank media (without cells) through polycarbonate filter as blank.
Flash-freeze tubes with liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C
Flash-freeze tubes with liquid nitrogen, store at -80 °C .

Fold the filter with two tweezers: (1) Fold in half along its diameter, creating a semicircular shape; (2) Fold once more in the same direction, resulting in a long strip; (3) For polycarbonate filter, fold once more, halving its length, so that sample is secured
Freeze-dry samples before extraction.
Protein extraction buffer (PEB, 4X)
20% Sarcosine Dilute two part 30% sarcosine with one part 50 mM pH 7 Tris buffer. N-lauroylsacosine sodium salt solution (30%) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #61747 (CAS: 137-16-6)
50 mM pH 7 Tris buffer Dilute five part 1M pH 7 Tris with 95 part MilliQ.
Protein extraction buffer (PEB, 1X): 2% SDS, 10% glycerol, 5 mM EDTA in 100 mM pH 7 Tris buffer:
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Keep tubes On ice for 00:01:00
Check labels. Put tubes back into FastPrep.
Turn on the refrigerate centrifuge, set temperature at 4 °C CENTRIFUGE 5430 R (Eppendorf, MP2231000510)
Centrifuging the extract. 20000 rcf, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Aliquot
Thermo Scientific™ Pierce™ Bovine Serum Albumin Standard 2 mg/mL (50 mL) Thermo Scientific Catalog #Thermo Scientific™ 0023210
Use a transfer pipet to add 40 g glycerol into a 100 mL reagent bottle. Glycerol Bioshop Catalog #GLY001.500 (CAS: 56-81-5)
Weigh 8 g SDS and transfer to the same reagent bottle Sodium dodecyl sulfate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L3771 (CAS: 151-21-3)
Add 0.5 M EDTA 4 mL EDTA buffer solution (0.5 M) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #4055-100ml (CAS: 6381-92-6)
Top to 100 g with 100 mM pH 7 Tris buffer.
Protein extract from go to step #39.2
Turn on refrigerate centrifuge, set temperature at 4 °C CENTRIFUGE 5430 R (Eppendorf, MP2231000510)
Add 300 µL MilliQ into each tube with extract.
In the fume hood, vortex after adding chloroform 100 µL Chloroform (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #439142-4L
In the fume hood, add methanol 400 µL Methanol (HPLC grade) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #34860-4X2L-R
Vortex until the mixture turns milky.
Gently vortex for 00:00:30 by using a tube insert VWR ANALOG VORTEX MIXER (VWR, 10153-838, With tube insert)
Incubate in the fridge for 00:30:00
20000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
In the fume hood, remove upper phase (about 700 uL) Do not disturb the interphase.
In the fume hood, add 300 µL methanol. Invert the tube gently at a slight angle to avoid letting precipitated protein and liquid touch the inside of the cap.
20000 rcf, 4 °C, 10 minutes
In the fume hood, remove all solvent by using 100 uL pipet tip. Watch the pipet tip closely. Do not remove protein pellets with the solvent.

With microtube lid open and vacuum pump running, dry pellet in vacuum desiccator for 00:15:00 at Room temperature Methanol and chloroform interfere the BCA assay. However, do not dry protein pellet for too long, otherwise it might lead to difficulties in resolubilizing the pellet .
Transfer about 800 uL 2 mg/mL BSA to a 2 mL microtube. Thermo Scientific™ Pierce™ Bovine Serum Albumin Standard 2 mg/mL (50 mL) Thermo Scientific Catalog #Thermo Scientific™ 0023210
BSA standard solutions %s %s
Vortex and then use reverse pipetting: transfer 100 µL standard solutions (PS2 to PS8) into the new tubes.
Use the following formula to determine the total volume of working reagent (WR) required. Consider leaving several mL of extra volume: (# standards + # samples) X 800 µL = total volume WR required
Prepare WR by mixing 50 parts of BCA reagent A with 1 part of BCA Reagent B in a 50 mL falcon tube Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #23225
Use one tip and reverse pipetting: Add 800 µL WR into each tube, make sure that the tip doesn't have contact with the solution, so that samples are not cross-contaminated.
Vortex each tube, shake and incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 at 200 rpm.
Remove samples from the incubator, and centrifuge 13300 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Calculation refers to the section: Calculate protein content per filter
Each microplate can hold eight standard solutions and forty samples+blanks, all in duplicate 96-Well Microplates (Polystyrene, Clear,, Greiner Bio-One, 82050-760)
Shake for 5 s at 600 rpm in a continuous and high force mode Read endpoint 562 nm with a measurement time 100 ms Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)
Rinse forceps with 95% ethanol and air dry Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)
Label Lysing matrix tubes (containing 1.4 mm ceramic spheres, 0.1 mm silica spheres and one 4 mm glass bead) and use clean forceps to transfer samples and blank filters into its corresponding tube.

Prior to transferring: (1) For filters folded into half-strip, unfold once to return to a strip. (2) For filters folded into quarter-circles, unfold once to return to a half-circle shape, then fold once along the dimension to form a strip. (2) For filters haphazardly into a compact mass, carefully unfold with two tweezers (avoiding losing biomass), fold once into a half-cricle shape, then fold once more along the dimension to form a strip
Insert the strip into beads mixture.
Place the bead tubes On ice .
Shake at 200 rpm, 37 °C, for complete dissolution. PEB (4X) might precipitate at room temperature due to the high concentration of glycerol and SDS. Redissolve by shaking at 37 degree.
Micro BCA Protein Assay Kit Thermo Scientific Catalog #23235
Reverse pipetting, add 100 µL RSB ( go to step #82 ) to each tube with precipitated protein ( go to step #81 ). Vortex for complete re-solubilization.
Re-solubilization buffer (RSB): Add 500 µL 20% sarcosine ( go to step #6 ) into 9.5 mL 50 mM Tris ( go to step #5 )
Store at -80 °C .
Prepare seven 2 mL microtubes for P2 to P8.
Organize eight 2 mL microtubes in the tube rack, label the tubes from P1 to P8, P denotes precipitated.
Reverse pipet 100 µL RSB ( go to step #82 ) into P1.
Standard curve
For crude protein ranging from 40 to 400 ug/mL, the standard curve is quadratic. Abs = aConc^2+bConc+c Where Abs is the blank corrected absorbance at 562 nm and Conc is the concentration of protein.

For precipitated protein or crude protein ranging from 0 to 40 ug/mL, the standard curve is linear. Abs=aConc+b Where Abs is the blank corrected absorbance at 562 nm and Conc is the concentration of protein.
Enter the standard deviation (SD) of the absorbance values from the blank as Abs in the standard curve to determine the Conc .
L-LOD=3.3*Conc
If the calculated sample concentration is lower than L-LOD, then use "<LOD" as the result, indicating the protein content is undetectable.
For microBCA assay Transfer 12 uL supernatant to 2 mL microtube
For protein precipitation Transfer 100 uL supernatant to 2 mL microtube in duplicate for protein precipitation.
Thaw protein extract ( go to step #39.1 )
4% PEB Mix: 1.6 mL PEB (1X) 38.4 mL MilliQ
Add 288 µL MQ into each extract.
Reverse pipet, load 100 µL diluted extract into the microplate, in duplicate.
Reverse pipetting: load 4 µL of standard solution (from C9 to C2) onto microdrop plate. µDrop™ Plates (Thermo Scientific, N12391) Concentrations of C10 to C12 are below detection limit.
Read absorbance of eight standard solutions at 205 nm.

Plot the Absorbance at 205 nm versus its concentration in mg/ml. If the standard curve has good Coefficient of Determination, i.e., R 2 >0.99, the standard solutions are in good quality; otherwise, prepare a new series of standard solutions until the quality of standard solutions meets the requirement.
Shake the 4X PEB at 37 °C until the SDS is fully dissolved back into the solution.
one part Protein extraction buffer (PEB, 4X) three part 100 mM pH 7 tris buffer
